last_completed_round.direction_name: Human idea 10 Stage: keep the corrected 3-stage hot band, but squeeze it under the 128-reg cliff
last_completed_round.hypothesis: Round 8 is the first correct 3-stage result, and the machine-level shift is too large to dismiss: barrier dropped from 14.30 to 4.60, long scoreboard from 6.21 to 1.21, mio from 2.30 to 0.71, registers/thread from 167 to 132, and shared/block from 46.6 KB to 44.8 KB. That says the Stage family is real, but it is not yet cashing out into runtime because tensor issue stayed middling and occupancy is still pinned at 1 block/SM. The next move should stay inside Idea 10 and target the concrete cliff that is now close enough to matter: trim stage/export bookkeeping live ranges so the hot 64x384 PTX kernel can get from 132 regs/thread to 128 or below without giving back the new low-stall signature. If that works, the Stage family finally has a plausible path to convert overlap into more resident work and real runtime wins.
